meaning: adj.柔韧性,易曲的,灵活的,柔软的,能变形的,可通融的
examples: The talks can succeed if both sides are flexible and serious.
You need to be more flexible and imaginative in your approach.
WYSIWYG editing makes your word processing smoother and more flexible.
This means open in their economies, eschewing protectionism, welcoming foreign investment, running flexible labour markets.
By honouring post-doctoral level women in science with flexible financial aid, the prize is completely unique.
